title: artemisia japonica var. japonica
intro: Artemisia japonica var. japonica, also known as Japanese mugwort or yomogi, is a species of perennial herb in the daisy family (Asteraceae) native to East Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antipyretic properties, which make it effective in treating fever, colds, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Japanese mugwort include its ability to aid digestion, relieve menstrual cramps, and reduce inflammation, making it a popular ingredient in traditional herbal remedies and teas. Japanese mugwort is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and can also be used in cooking and as a natural dye, showcasing its versatility in both medicinal and culinary applications.
